verdict.answer_first: A leads harder: "it's a timezone problem"; B leads with weaker hedge: "almost certainly a timezone boundary bug".
verdict.decisiveness: A is more decisive on the diagnosis, but B is cleaner on the one prescription: "store and render it as a plain YYYY-MM-DD" for date-only values.
verdict.fabrication: A overstates/invents context: "single most common date bug" and "your US users complain and your European users don't"; B: none.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to execute because it gives a concrete confirmation path: "change your machine's timezone to America/Los_Angeles" and inspect "T00:00:00Z".
